scenarios.reply_summary: Reply mentions skills, agents, chat capabilities, web search, file operations — helpful, no error.
scenarios.notes: Router answered inline (chat_turn_completed_inline). No artifact requirement. search_actions not called.

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply explains idempotency: same operation multiple times yields same result, cites HTTP GET/PUT/DELETE/HEAD.
scenarios.notes: Router called web__search (hot_list_alias). Empty stop after tool_returned; REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY retry fired and produced valid reply. search_actions not called. No artifact requirement — rubric met.

scenarios.reply_summary: Lists 14 skill names with descriptions (direct_llm, eval, eval_builder, index_docs, index_events, judge_phase, ops_report, simple_memo_app, skill_builder, skill_importer, skill_improver, skill_search, word_stats_demo, writing_review_app).
scenarios.notes: must_emit_any satisfied via chat_turn_completed_inline. Router used search_actions tool to list skills (tool_called observed), then answered inline. search_actions was called here to enumerate skills.

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply states text contains 44 characters, 1 line, longest line 44 chars, ~11 tokens — word/char stats present.
scenarios.notes: skill_run_completed with status=finished emitted. word_stats_demo workflow_finished with artifact text_review confirmed. Empty stop after spawn ack; retry + skill_completion_inject resolved. search_actions not called.

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply asked for user preference instead of writing a poem: 'はい、どのような詩がお好みですか？' — no poem content.
scenarios.notes: Rubric requires poem text with >=2 lines — not met. Artifacts require direct_llm skill present — not met (inline only). Router deflected with clarification question instead of writing poem. Same failure pattern as B55.

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 2 reply provides Python list comprehension code examples (squares, even_numbers_doubled) coherent with turn 1.
scenarios.notes: Multi-turn pronoun reference ('それを') resolved correctly to list comprehension. Both turns answered inline. No artifact requirement. search_actions not called.

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply offered to generate an image: 'どのような画像を生成しますか？' — did not decline, pretended capability exists.
scenarios.notes: Rubric requires polite decline and not pretending to generate an image — both items violated. Router answered inline offering to proceed with image generation. Same failure pattern as B55.

new_findings.title: search_actions not called in any W1 scenario despite --eager-embedding-build
new_findings.description: All 7 scenarios ran with --eager-embedding-build enabled (search_actions should be in tools[] from Turn 1). No scenario triggered a search_actions tool call. S3 (skill_discovery_request) used a tool_called event but it resolved via a different path (inline tool, not search_actions by name). Conclusion: search_actions embedding was built but the router did not invoke it in any W1 scenario. W3/W5/W6 are more likely candidates per the batch note.

new_findings.title: S5 poem-refusal attractor persists (B55 carry-over)
new_findings.description: S5 catalog_routing_decided_emitted: router deflected with 'どのような詩がお好みですか？' instead of writing a poem. This is the same R verdict observed in B55. The attractor (router asks for clarification rather than directly answering creative requests) persists across batches.

new_findings.title: S7 image-generation hallucination persists (B55 carry-over)
new_findings.description: S7 out_of_scope_graceful_decline: router offered to generate an image rather than declining. Reply: 'どのような画像を生成しますか？'. This violates both rubric items (polite decline + not pretending capability). Same R verdict as B55.

new_findings.title: S2 empty-stop after web__search tool return — REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY resolved
new_findings.description: S2 factual_query_direct_llm: router called web__search (hot_list_alias routing), then produced an empty stop (finish_reason=stop, completion_tokens=0). REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 retry injected directive (176 chars) and the subsequent LLM call produced a valid factual answer. Final verdict V. This is expected behavior for the retry mechanism.
